title: quercus humilis subsp. lanuginosa
intro: Quercus humilis subsp. lanuginosa, also known as the Downy Oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antioxidant. The health benefits of Quercus humilis subsp. lanuginosa include its potential to help lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support overall well-being and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
